## Phase 4: Design System Enforcement

### 4.1 Create Design System Hook
**File:** `.claude/hooks/enforce-design-system.sh`

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# Enforce BlueprintJS design system
# Block: custom colors, non-BP components, arbitrary spacing

BLOCKED_PATTERNS=(
  "style={{.*color:"
  "style={{.*background:"
  "import.*from.*@patternfly"
  "import.*from.*@mui"
  "import.*from.*antd"
)

for pattern in "${BLOCKED_PATTERNS[@]}"; do
  if grep -rE "$pattern" apps/canvas/src/ 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "BLOCKED: Design system violation - use BlueprintJS tokens"
    exit 1
  fi
done
```

### 4.2 Update CLAUDE.md with Design System Section

Add section:
```markdown
## Design System (BlueprintJS 6)

### Color Usage
- Use Blueprint Intent colors: PRIMARY, SUCCESS, WARNING, DANGER
- Dark theme tokens: `bp6-dark` class
- Task types: PREVENT (green), CORRECT (orange), OVERHAUL (red)

### Component Mapping
| Need | BlueprintJS Component |
|------|----------------------|
| Navbar | `<Navbar>` + `<NavbarGroup>` |
| Sidebar | `<Card>` with elevation |
| Buttons | `<Button>` / `<ButtonGroup>` |
| Tags | `<Tag>` with intent |
| Panels | `<Card>` / `<Drawer>` |
| Inputs | `<InputGroup>` |
| Selects | `<Select>` / `<HTMLSelect>` |

### Spacing Scale (4px base)
- xs: 4px, sm: 8px, md: 12px, lg: 16px, xl: 24px

### Typography
- Use Blueprint's built-in typography classes
- Monospace for train IDs: `bp6-monospace-text`
```

    87→---
    88→
    89→# PART I: DOMAIN MODEL
    90→
    91→## 1. Railway Maintenance Domain
    92→
    93→### 1.1 ECM Framework (EU 2019/779)
    94→
    95→The **Entity in Charge of Maintenance (ECM)** framework establishes legal responsibilities for railway vehicle maintenance. While Dubai Metro is outside EU jurisdiction, the ECM framework represents industry best practice.
    96→
    97→#### ECM Function Mapping
    98→
    99→| Function | Responsibility | MMS V1.0 Feature | Status |
   100→|----------|----------------|------------------|--------|
   101→| **ECM-F1** | Maintenance Management | Dashboard KPIs, compliance tracking | Visualization only |
   102→| **ECM-F2** | Maintenance Development | Interval definitions, plan visualization | Mock data display |
   103→| **ECM-F3** | Fleet Maintenance Management | Real-time fleet status, conflict detection | Conflict indicators |
   104→| **ECM-F4** | Maintenance Delivery | Work order visualization, depot allocation | Visual representation |
   105→
   106→#### ECM Compliance Requirements
   107→
   108→| Requirement | ECM Article | MMS V1.0 Implementation |
   109→|-------------|-------------|------------------------|
   110→| Risk assessment | Art. 5.2 | Conflict detection, overdue warnings |
   111→| Competence management | Art. 5.3 | Not implemented |
   112→| Documentation | Art. 5.4 | Not implemented |
   113→| Performance monitoring | Art. 5.5 | KPI display |
   114→
   115→---
   116→
   117→### 1.2 RAMS Compliance (EN 50126-1)
   118→
   119→**RAMS** = Reliability, Availability, Maintainability, Safety
   120→
   121→EN 50126-1:2017 defines the railway RAMS lifecycle. MMS visualizes RAMS metrics.
   122→
   123→#### RAMS Metric Definitions
   124→
   125→| Aspect | Definition | MMS V1.0 Feature |
   126→|--------|------------|------------------|
   127→| **Reliability** | Probability of intended function under stated conditions | MTBF display |
   128→| **Availability** | Ability to perform required function at given instant | Fleet availability % |
   129→| **Maintainability** | Probability of maintenance within stated time | MTTR display |
   130→| **Safety** | Freedom from unacceptable risk | Critical task priority |
   131→
   132→#### RAMS Targets
   133→
   134→| Metric | Formula | Target | Critical Threshold |
   135→|--------|---------|--------|-------------------|
   136→| Fleet Availability | (Fit Trains / Total Trains) x 100 | >= 97% | < 95% |
   137→| MTBF | Total Operating Hours / Number of Failures | Operator-defined | Operator-defined |
   138→| MTTR | Total Downtime Hours / Number of Repairs | <= 24 hours | > 48 hours |
   139→| Schedule Adherence | (On-Time / Scheduled) x 100 | >= 95% | < 90% |
   140→
   141→---
   142→
   143→### 1.3 EN 15380 Railway Terminology
   144→
   145→All user-facing labels MUST use EN 15380 compliant terminology.
   146→
   147→#### Mandatory Term Usage
   148→
   149→| EN 15380 Term | Definition | NOT Acceptable |
   150→|---------------|------------|----------------|
   151→| Rolling Stock | Railway vehicles requiring maintenance | Vehicle, Asset, Unit |
   152→| Fleet | Group of rolling stock sharing manufacturer/model | Group, Class, Collection |
   153→| Depot | Designated maintenance facility | Garage, Workshop, Station |
   154→| Train Fitness | Binary operational readiness (fit/unfit) | Availability, Health, Status |
   155→| Run Assignment | Daily allocation of train IDs to routes | Dispatch, Schedule, Trip |
   156→| Dead Running | Non-revenue movement between locations | Empty movement, Repositioning |
   157→| Mileage Balancing | Strategy to equalize wear across fleet | Usage balancing |
   158→
   159→#### Maintenance Terminology
   160→
   161→| Term | Definition | Context |
   162→|------|------------|---------|
   163→| PM (Preventive Maintenance) | Scheduled maintenance at defined intervals | Time or mileage-based |
   164→| CM (Corrective Maintenance) | Unscheduled repair after failure detection | Reactive |
   165→| CBM (Condition-Based Maintenance) | Triggered by condition monitoring | Sensor-based |
   166→| Overhaul | Major refurbishment restoring to original condition | Resets counters |
   167→| Heavy Maintenance | Significant maintenance requiring extended depot stay | Multi-day duration |
   168→
   169→---
   170→
   171→## 2. Dubai Metro Context
   172→
   173→### 2.1 Fleet Composition
   174→
   175→| Fleet | Prefix | Count | Line | Depot | Daily Avg km |
   176→|-------|--------|-------|------|-------|--------------|
   177→| Alstom | ALT-001 to ALT-079 | 79 | Red Line | Al Rashidiya | 420-475 km/day |
   178→| Kinki Sharyo | KNK-001 to KNK-049 | 49 | Green Line | Jebel Ali | 400-454 km/day |
   179→| **Total** | | **128** | | | |
   180→
   181→#### Alstom Fleet Characteristics
   182→
   183→| Property | Value |

# PART I: DOMAIN MODEL

## 1. Railway Maintenance Domain

### 1.1 ECM Framework (EU 2019/779)

The **Entity in Charge of Maintenance (ECM)** framework establishes legal responsibilities for railway vehicle maintenance. While Dubai Metro is outside EU jurisdiction, the ECM framework represents industry best practice.

#### ECM Function Mapping

| Function | Responsibility | MMS V1.0 Feature | Status |
|----------|----------------|------------------|--------|
| **ECM-F1** | Maintenance Management | Dashboard KPIs, compliance tracking | Visualization only |
| **ECM-F2** | Maintenance Development | Interval definitions, plan visualization | Mock data display |
| **ECM-F3** | Fleet Maintenance Management | Real-time fleet status, conflict detection | Conflict indicators |
| **ECM-F4** | Maintenance Delivery | Work order visualization, depot allocation | Visual representation |

#### ECM Compliance Requirements

| Requirement | ECM Article | MMS V1.0 Implementation |
|-------------|-------------|------------------------|
| Risk assessment | Art. 5.2 | Conflict detection, overdue warnings |
| Competence management | Art. 5.3 | Not implemented |
| Documentation | Art. 5.4 | Not implemented |
| Performance monitoring | Art. 5.5 | KPI display |

---

### 1.2 RAMS Compliance (EN 50126-1)

**RAMS** = Reliability, Availability, Maintainability, Safety

EN 50126-1:2017 defines the railway RAMS lifecycle. MMS visualizes RAMS metrics.

#### RAMS Metric Definitions

| Aspect | Definition | MMS V1.0 Feature |
|--------|------------|------------------|
| **Reliability** | Probability of intended function under stated conditions | MTBF display |
| **Availability** | Ability to perform required function at given instant | Fleet availability % |
| **Maintainability** | Probability of maintenance within stated time | MTTR display |
| **Safety** | Freedom from unacceptable risk | Critical task priority |

#### RAMS Targets

| Metric | Formula | Target | Critical Threshold |
|--------|---------|--------|-------------------|
| Fleet Availability | (Fit Trains / Total Trains) x 100 | >= 97% | < 95% |
| MTBF | Total Operating Hours / Number of Failures | Operator-defined | Operator-defined |
| MTTR | Total Downtime Hours / Number of Repairs | <= 24 hours | > 48 hours |
| Schedule Adherence | (On-Time / Scheduled) x 100 | >= 95% | < 90% |

---

### 1.3 EN 15380 Railway Terminology

All user-facing labels MUST use EN 15380 compliant terminology.

#### Mandatory Term Usage

| EN 15380 Term | Definition | NOT Acceptable |
|---------------|------------|----------------|
| Rolling Stock | Railway vehicles requiring maintenance | Vehicle, Asset, Unit |
| Fleet | Group of rolling stock sharing manufacturer/model | Group, Class, Collection |
| Depot | Designated maintenance facility | Garage, Workshop, Station |
| Train Fitness | Binary operational readiness (fit/unfit) | Availability, Health, Status |
| Run Assignment | Daily allocation of train IDs to routes | Dispatch, Schedule, Trip |
| Dead Running | Non-revenue movement between locations | Empty movement, Repositioning |
| Mileage Balancing | Strategy to equalize wear across fleet | Usage balancing |

#### Maintenance Terminology

| Term | Definition | Context |
|------|------------|---------|
| PM (Preventive Maintenance) | Scheduled maintenance at defined intervals | Time or mileage-based |
| CM (Corrective Maintenance) | Unscheduled repair after failure detection | Reactive |
| CBM (Condition-Based Maintenance) | Triggered by condition monitoring | Sensor-based |
| Overhaul | Major refurbishment restoring to original condition | Resets counters |
| Heavy Maintenance | Significant maintenance requiring extended depot stay | Multi-day duration |

---

## Session: 2026-01-05 (TOOLKIT-PERF-001: Complete Performance Audit) - COMPLETE ✅

### @zazcart/tools Critical Performance Fixes - ALL COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Complete audit and fix all performance issues in @zazcart/tools state machine library identified in FIXME files.

**Fixes Applied (7 total):**

1. **Unsafe Listener Unsubscription** (store.ts)
   - Copy listeners array before iteration to prevent issues during unsubscribe
   - Prevents ConcurrentModificationException-like bugs

2. **Validation on Hot Path** (context.ts)
   - Skip schema validation in production for performance
   - Only validate in development (NODE_ENV !== 'production')

3. **devLog Crash Risk** (logs.ts)
   - Safe error handling instead of throwing in devLog

4. **O(N²) reduceObject** (object-utils.ts)
   - Changed from spread operator in loop to Object.assign
   - O(N) implementation now

5. **GC Thrashing - Metadata Option** (machine.ts, models/machine.ts)
   - Added `metadata?: boolean` option to MachineConfig
   - When `false`, skips metadata updates on dispatch
   - Prevents GC thrashing from object allocations at 60fps

6. **V8 De-optimization** (hooks.ts)
   - Clean object return instead of hybrid array+object
   - Breaking change: `useMachine()` returns `{ state, actions, dispatch }`

7. **Double Proxying** (machine.ts)
   - Direct assignment instead of Immer produce() for selectors
   - Reduces proxy overhead in hot path

**FIXME Files Deleted:**
- packages/toolkit/FIXME_STATE_MACHINE.md
- packages/toolkit/FIXME_HELPERS.md
- packages/toolkit/FIXME_SCHEMA.md
- packages/toolkit/src/state-machine/FIXME.md

**Canvas Integration:**
- Applied `metadata: false` to canvas-machine.ts for 60fps optimization

**TypeScript Fixes:**
- Fixed reduceObject return type in object-utils.ts
- Fixed check() return type in context.ts

**Tests:**
- 216 pass, 4 fail (pre-existing stack trace test failures in error-utils)

**QA Visual Verification (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅ App running at 120fps (green FPS counter visible)
- ✅ All LOD levels rendering correctly (MACRO/MESO/MICRO)
- ✅ Sidebar with 50 trains visible
- ✅ Minimap functional
- ✅ Timeline header with dates
- ✅ No console errors

**Files Modified:**
- packages/toolkit/src/state-machine/store.ts
- packages/toolkit/src/state-machine/context.ts
- packages/toolkit/src/state-machine/machine.ts
- packages/toolkit/src/state-machine/models/machine.ts
- packages/toolkit/src/state-machine/hooks.ts
- packages/toolkit/src/helpers/logs.ts
- packages/toolkit/src/helpers/object-utils.ts
- apps/canvas/src/state/canvas-machine.ts
- apps/canvas/src/state/viewport.ts
- apps/canvas/src/state/interaction.ts

**Impact:**
- Eliminated GC thrashing for high-frequency state machines
- V8 hidden class optimization for better JIT performance
- O(N) instead of O(N²) for object reduction
- Production-only validation for performance
- All features still passing (53 complete)

- ~120 lines of ad-hoc code replaced with D3 modules
- Improved cross-browser compatibility
- Perceptually uniform color scales
- Better maintainability with standard library
- Bundle increase: ~20KB gzipped

**Next Task:** STATE-ARCH-001 (High-Performance State Architecture) is now unblocked

---

### D3-MIGRATE-001 Phase 1: Quick Wins - COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Replace ad-hoc utility implementations with battle-tested D3 modules.

**Implementation Completed:**

1. **d3-array Integration (types.ts):**
   - Installed: `bun add d3-array @types/d3-array`
   - Replaced manual extent/max iterations with `d3-array.extent()` and `d3-array.max()`
   - Used in `calculateDataBounds()` function
   - ~20 lines reduced

2. **d3-time/d3-time-format Integration (TimelineHeader.tsx):**
   - Installed: `bun add d3-time d3-time-format @types/d3-time @types/d3-time-format`
   - Replaced `addDays()` with `timeDay.offset()`
   - Replaced `getUnitStart()` with `timeIntervals[granularity].floor()`
   - Replaced `advanceUnit()` with `timeIntervals[granularity].offset()`
   - Replaced date format functions with `timeFormat()` patterns
   - ~50 lines reduced

3. **d3-scale/d3-interpolate Integration (Minimap.tsx):**
   - Installed: `bun add d3-scale d3-interpolate @types/d3-scale @types/d3-interpolate`
   - Replaced `worldToMinimap()` with `scaleLinear()` coordinate conversion
   - Replaced `minimapToWorld()` with `scaleLinear().invert()`
   - Replaced `interpolateColor()` with `interpolateRgb()`
   - ~30 lines reduced

**Files Modified:**
- `apps/canvas/package.json` (new dependencies)
- `apps/canvas/src/data/types.ts` (d3-array)
- `apps/canvas/src/components/TimelineHeader.tsx` (d3-time, d3-time-format)
- `apps/canvas/src/components/Minimap.tsx` (d3-scale, d3-interpolate)

**Commit:**
- `0f260d4` feat(d3): D3-MIGRATE-001 Phase 1 - integrate d3 modules for core utilities

**Visual Verification (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅ App renders correctly at localhost:5173
- ✅ FPS: 120 (avg: 119) maintained
- ✅ Minimap heatmap renders with correct color gradient
- ✅ Minimap viewport rectangle positioned correctly
- ✅ TimelineHeader date labels render at all zoom levels
- ✅ No console errors

**Impact:**
- ~100 lines of ad-hoc code replaced with D3 modules
- Improved cross-browser compatibility
- Better maintainability with standard library
- Bundle increase: ~15KB gzipped (d3-array, d3-time, d3-time-format, d3-scale, d3-interpolate)

**Next Steps:**
- Phase 2: d3-zoom integration for InfiniteCanvas (may resolve BUG-009 residual issues)
- Phase 3: d3-scale-chromatic for MacroLayer heatmap

---

## Session: 2025-12-17 (PERF-002 Completion) - COMPLETE ✅

### PERF-002: Viewport Culling & Spatial Indexing - FOUNDATION COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Implement spatial indexing infrastructure for viewport culling optimization.

**Implementation Completed:**

1. **Phase 0 - BUG-009 Fix (Previous Session):**
   - Trackpad zoom lag fixed by applying zoom directly without accumulation/animation
   - Verified 120fps during pinch-to-zoom

2. **Phase 1 - Spatial Index Interface:**
   - Created `apps/canvas/src/spatial/types.ts`:
     - ISpatialIndex interface with load, insert, remove, queryViewport, queryRadius, queryNearest
     - ViewportBounds, SpatialItem, SpatialQueryResult types
     - Helper functions: taskToSpatialItem, tasksToSpatialItems, rectanglesIntersect
   - Created `apps/canvas/src/spatial/SimpleSpatialIndex.ts`:
     - O(n) placeholder implementation using Map<string, SpatialItem>
     - Linear scan for all queries (will be replaced with RBush in STATE-ARCH-001)

3. **Phase 2 - Culled Instance Hooks:**
   - Created `apps/canvas/src/rendering/useCulledInstances.ts`:
     - useCulledInstances: Reorders tasks so visible ones are at front
     - calculateViewportBounds: Converts camera + canvas size to world bounds
     - useStableViewportBounds: Memoized bounds with padding
   - Created `apps/canvas/src/rendering/index.ts` for re-exports

4. **Phase 3 - LOD Layer Integration:**
   - Created `apps/canvas/src/spatial/useSpatialIndex.ts`:
     - useSpatialIndex hook creates and maintains spatial index
     - useVisibleTaskIds hook queries visible task IDs
     - Auto-rebuilds when tasks change
   - Updated `InfiniteCanvas.tsx`:
     - Added useSpatialIndex hook
     - Pass spatialIndex to LODLayers
   - Updated `MesoLayer.tsx`:
     - Added viewportBounds and spatialIndex props
     - Props plumbing ready for actual culling optimization

5. **Phase 4 - Performance Validation:**
   - FPS: 120 (avg: 120) verified via screenshot
   - SpatialIndex loads 4982 items in 0.40ms
   - All LOD levels render correctly (MACRO, MESO, MICRO)
   - No console errors

**Files Created:**
- `apps/canvas/src/spatial/types.ts`
- `apps/canvas/src/spatial/SimpleSpatialIndex.ts`
- `apps/canvas/src/spatial/useSpatialIndex.ts`
- `apps/canvas/src/spatial/index.ts`
- `apps/canvas/src/rendering/useCulledInstances.ts`
- `apps/canvas/src/rendering/index.ts`

**Files Modified:**
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (spatial index integration)
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/layers/MesoLayer.tsx` (viewport/spatial props)
- `features.json` (PERF-002 passes: true)

**Deferred to STATE-ARCH-001:**
- Actual culling optimization (mesh.count manipulation)
- RBush R-tree replacing SimpleSpatialIndex
- TypedArray-backed spatial data

**Visual Verification (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅ MACRO (0.44x): Heatmap renders correctly
- ✅ MESO (1.00x): Task blocks with row backgrounds
- ✅ MICRO (2.25x): Task cards with labels and dependency lines"
"content":"# MMS Project Realignment Plan

## Executive Summary

This plan addresses the cleanup and realignment of the MMS (Metro Mileage Management System) project at `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/mms/system`, incorporating valuable UX research from the reference project at `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/mms-last-attempt`.

**Key Finding**: The current project is MORE advanced (53 completed features vs 27 in reference), but the reference has superior UX research and documentation that should be ported.

---

## Current State Analysis

### Main Project (mms/system)
| Aspect | Status |
|--------|--------|
| Features Complete | 53 of 53 (100%) |
| Stack | React 18.3.1 + BlueprintJS 6.1.0 + R3F 8.17 + Three.js 0.170 |
| State Management | @zazcart/tools state machine |
| Spatial Indexing | RBush (O(log n) queries) |
| Performance | 120fps verified |
| Storybook | **NONE** (no cleanup needed) |

### Reference Project (mms-last-attempt)
| Aspect | Status |
|--------|--------|
| Features Complete | 27 of 27 |
| Stack | React 19 + PatternFly 6 + R3F 9 + Three.js 0.170 |
| UX Research | **EXCELLENT** - comprehensive competitor analysis, screen specs |
| Documentation | Superior `_memory/` folder with ADRs and design system |

---

## Issues Identified

### A. Dead Code & Artifacts (REMOVE)
| Item | Path | Action |
|------|------|--------|
| Genesis attempt logs | `genesis/attempt6202/chats/conversations.jsonl` | DELETE |
| Genesis directory | `genesis/` | DELETE entire directory |

### B. Stale Comments (UPDATE)
| File | Issue |
|------|-------|
| `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` | Lines 13-14 reference removed PatternFly migration |
| `apps/canvas/src/components/TimelineHeader.tsx` | Line 13 same stale comment |

### C. Documentation Inconsistencies (CONSOLIDATE)
| File | Issue | Action |
|------|-------|--------|
| `docs/SPECIFICATION.md` (63KB) | Original authoritative spec | KEEP as engineering reference |
| `docs/MMS_COMPLETE_SPECIFICATION.md` (295KB) | Extended version | REVIEW for consolidation |
| `docs/RESEARCH.md` | May reference old decisions | ARCHIVE to `docs/archive/` |
| `docs/PARALLEL_EXECUTION.md` | Old execution strategy | ARCHIVE |

### D. Missing UX Research (PORT FROM REFERENCE)
| Source File | Target Location | Content |
|-------------|-----------------|---------|
| `_memory/ux-competitor-analysis.md` | `docs/ux/competitor-analysis.md` | Competitor analysis (Maximo, Railnova, etc.) |
| `_memory/ux-screens.md` | `docs/ux/screens.md` | 7 screen specifications |
| `_memory/ux-charts.md` | `docs/ux/charts.md` | 9 chart type specifications |
| `_memory/ux-navigation.md` | `docs/ux/navigation.md` | User journeys, keyboard shortcuts |
| `_memory/ux-unknown-unknowns.md` | `docs/ux/unknown-unknowns.md` | Hidden requirements analysis |
| `_memory/design-system.md` | `docs/ux/design-system.md` | Color palette, tokens |
| `_memory/architecture-decisions.md` | `docs/architecture-decisions.md` | 5 ADRs |

---

## Realignment Tasks

### Phase 1: Cleanup Dead Code (15 min)
1. Delete `genesis/` directory entirely
2. Remove stale comments from InfiniteCanvas.tsx and TimelineHeader.tsx
3. Verify no broken imports after deletion

### Phase 2: Archive Stale Documentation (10 min)
1. Create `docs/archive/` directory
2. Move potentially outdated research docs:
   - `docs/RESEARCH.md` → `docs/archive/`
   - `docs/PARALLEL_EXECUTION.md` → `docs/archive/`
3. Update any internal links

### Phase 3: Port UX Research (30 min)
1. Create `docs/ux/` directory
2. Copy and adapt all `_memory/` files from reference project:
   - ux-competitor-analysis.md
   - ux-screens.md
   - ux-charts.md
   - ux-navigation.md
   - ux-unknown-unknowns.md
   - design-system.md
3. Copy architecture-decisions.md to docs/
4. Update file headers to reference current stack (BlueprintJS, not PatternFly)

### Phase 4: Consolidate Harness Files (15 min)
1. Review and update `features.json`:
   - Remove any stale/orphan feature entries
   - Ensure all 53 features are properly documented
2. Summarize `progress.txt`:
   - Archive historical sessions older than 30 days
   - Keep recent progress visible
3. Update `CLAUDE.md` with any new learnings

### Phase 5: Verification (20 min)
1. Run typecheck: `bun run typecheck`
2. Start dev server: `cd apps/canvas && bun run dev`
3. Visual verification via Playwright MCP:
   - All LOD levels render (MACRO/MESO/MICRO)
   - No console errors
   - 60fps+ maintained
4. Commit changes with descriptive message

---

## Stack Decision (REQUIRES CLARIFICATION)

### Current Divergence
| Aspect | Main Project | Reference |
|--------|--------------|-----------|
| React | 18.3.1 | 19.x |
| UI Library | BlueprintJS 6.1.0 | PatternFly 6 |
| R3F | 8.17.x | 9.x |

### Options
1. **KEEP Current Stack** (Recommended)
   - React 18 is stable with BlueprintJS 6
   - 53 features already working
   - Upgrade risk outweighs benefits for V1.0

2. **Upgrade to Reference Stack**
   - React 19 + PatternFly 6 + R3F 9
   - Breaking changes in all three
   - Would require significant refactoring

---

## Verification Checklist

After implementation:
- [ ] `genesis/` directory deleted
- [ ] No stale framework references in comments
- [ ] `docs/ux/` contains all research from reference
- [ ] `docs/archive/` contains old research docs
- [ ] Typecheck passes
- [ ] Visual verification passes (3 LOD levels)
- [ ] No console errors
- [ ] 60fps+ maintained
- [ ] features.json updated
- [ ] progress.txt updated
- [ ] Git commit created

---

## Files to Modify

### DELETE
- `genesis/` (entire directory)

### UPDATE
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (remove stale comments)
- `apps/canvas/src/components/TimelineHeader.tsx` (remove stale comments)
- `features.json` (cleanup if needed)
- `progress.txt` (summarize old sessions)
- `CLAUDE.md` (if needed)

### CREATE
- `docs/ux/competitor-analysis.md`
- `docs/ux/screens.md`
- `docs/ux/charts.md`
- `docs/ux/navigation.md`
- `docs/ux/unknown-unknowns.md`
- `docs/ux/design-system.md`
- `docs/architecture-decisions.md`
- `docs/archive/` (directory)

### MOVE
- `docs/RESEARCH.md` → `docs/archive/RESEARCH.md`
- `docs/PARALLEL_EXECUTION.md` → `docs/archive/PARALLEL_EXECUTION.md`

---

## Estimated Time: ~90 minutes

| Phase | Duration |
|-------|----------|
| Phase 1: Cleanup | 15 min |
| Phase 2: Archive | 10 min |
| Phase 3: Port UX | 30 min |
| Phase 4: Harness | 15 min |
| Phase 5: Verify | 20 min |

---

## Post-Realignment State

After this plan is executed:
1. Project will have NO dead code or artifacts
2. All valuable UX research will be consolidated in `docs/ux/`
3. Stale documentation will be archived (not deleted, for reference)
4. Harness files will be clean and current
5. Project will be ready for continued development on V1.0 features

# PART I: DOMAIN MODEL

## 1. Railway Maintenance Domain

### 1.1 ECM Framework (EU 2019/779)

The **Entity in Charge of Maintenance (ECM)** framework establishes legal responsibilities for railway vehicle maintenance. While Dubai Metro is outside EU jurisdiction, the ECM framework represents industry best practice.

#### ECM Function Mapping

| Function | Responsibility | MMS V1.0 Feature | Status |
|----------|----------------|------------------|--------|
| **ECM-F1** | Maintenance Management | Dashboard KPIs, compliance tracking | Visualization only |
| **ECM-F2** | Maintenance Development | Interval definitions, plan visualization | Mock data display |
| **ECM-F3** | Fleet Maintenance Management | Real-time fleet status, conflict detection | Conflict indicators |
| **ECM-F4** | Maintenance Delivery | Work order visualization, depot allocation | Visual representation |

#### ECM Compliance Requirements

| Requirement | ECM Article | MMS V1.0 Implementation |
|-------------|-------------|------------------------|
| Risk assessment | Art. 5.2 | Conflict detection, overdue warnings |
| Competence management | Art. 5.3 | Not implemented |
| Documentation | Art. 5.4 | Not implemented |
| Performance monitoring | Art. 5.5 | KPI display |

---

### 1.2 RAMS Compliance (EN 50126-1)

**RAMS** = Reliability, Availability, Maintainability, Safety

EN 50126-1:2017 defines the railway RAMS lifecycle. MMS visualizes RAMS metrics.

#### RAMS Metric Definitions

| Aspect | Definition | MMS V1.0 Feature |
|--------|------------|------------------|
| **Reliability** | Probability of intended function under stated conditions | MTBF display |
| **Availability** | Ability to perform required function at given instant | Fleet availability % |
| **Maintainability** | Probability of maintenance within stated time | MTTR display |
| **Safety** | Freedom from unacceptable risk | Critical task priority |

#### RAMS Targets

| Metric | Formula | Target | Critical Threshold |
|--------|---------|--------|-------------------|
| Fleet Availability | (Fit Trains / Total Trains) x 100 | >= 97% | < 95% |
| MTBF | Total Operating Hours / Number of Failures | Operator-defined | Operator-defined |
| MTTR | Total Downtime Hours / Number of Repairs | <= 24 hours | > 48 hours |
| Schedule Adherence | (On-Time / Scheduled) x 100 | >= 95% | < 90% |

---

### 1.3 EN 15380 Railway Terminology

All user-facing labels MUST use EN 15380 compliant terminology.

#### Mandatory Term Usage

| EN 15380 Term | Definition | NOT Acceptable |
|---------------|------------|----------------|
| Rolling Stock | Railway vehicles requiring maintenance | Vehicle, Asset, Unit |
| Fleet | Group of rolling stock sharing manufacturer/model | Group, Class, Collection |
| Depot | Designated maintenance facility | Garage, Workshop, Station |
| Train Fitness | Binary operational readiness (fit/unfit) | Availability, Health, Status |
| Run Assignment | Daily allocation of train IDs to routes | Dispatch, Schedule, Trip |
| Dead Running | Non-revenue movement between locations | Empty movement, Repositioning |
| Mileage Balancing | Strategy to equalize wear across fleet | Usage balancing |

#### Maintenance Terminology

| Term | Definition | Context |
|------|------------|---------|
| PM (Preventive Maintenance) | Scheduled maintenance at defined intervals | Time or mileage-based |
| CM (Corrective Maintenance) | Unscheduled repair after failure detection | Reactive |
| CBM (Condition-Based Maintenance) | Triggered by condition monitoring | Sensor-based |
| Overhaul | Major refurbishment restoring to original condition | Resets counters |
| Heavy Maintenance | Significant maintenance requiring extended depot stay | Multi-day duration |

---

| Kinki Sharyo | KNK-001 to KNK-049 | 49 | Green Line | Jebel Ali | 400-454 km/day |

## Session: 2026-01-05 (TOOLKIT-PERF-001: Complete Performance Audit) - COMPLETE ✅

### @zazcart/tools Critical Performance Fixes - ALL COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Complete audit and fix all performance issues in @zazcart/tools state machine library identified in FIXME files.

**Fixes Applied (7 total):**

1. **Unsafe Listener Unsubscription** (store.ts)
   - Copy listeners array before iteration to prevent issues during unsubscribe
   - Prevents ConcurrentModificationException-like bugs

2. **Validation on Hot Path** (context.ts)
   - Skip schema validation in production for performance
   - Only validate in development (NODE_ENV !== 'production')

3. **devLog Crash Risk** (logs.ts)
   - Safe error handling instead of throwing in devLog

4. **O(N²) reduceObject** (object-utils.ts)
   - Changed from spread operator in loop to Object.assign
   - O(N) implementation now

5. **GC Thrashing - Metadata Option** (machine.ts, models/machine.ts)
   - Added `metadata?: boolean` option to MachineConfig
   - When `false`, skips metadata updates on dispatch
   - Prevents GC thrashing from object allocations at 60fps

6. **V8 De-optimization** (hooks.ts)
   - Clean object return instead of hybrid array+object
   - Breaking change: `useMachine()` returns `{ state, actions, dispatch }`

7. **Double Proxying** (machine.ts)
   - Direct assignment instead of Immer produce() for selectors
   - Reduces proxy overhead in hot path

"content":"# MMS Project Realignment Plan

## Executive Summary

This plan addresses the cleanup and realignment of the MMS (Metro Mileage Management System) project at `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/mms/system`, incorporating valuable UX research from the reference project at `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/mms-last-attempt`.

**Key Finding**: The current project is MORE advanced (53 completed features vs 27 in reference), but the reference has superior UX research and documentation that should be ported.

---

## Current State Analysis

### Main Project (mms/system)
| Aspect | Status |
|--------|--------|
| Features Complete | 53 of 53 (100%) |
| Stack | React 18.3.1 + BlueprintJS 6.1.0 + R3F 8.17 + Three.js 0.170 |
| State Management | @zazcart/tools state machine |
| Spatial Indexing | RBush (O(log n) queries) |
| Performance | 120fps verified |
| Storybook | **NONE** (no cleanup needed) |

### Reference Project (mms-last-attempt)
| Aspect | Status |
|--------|--------|
| Features Complete | 27 of 27 |
| Stack | React 19 + PatternFly 6 + R3F 9 + Three.js 0.170 |
| UX Research | **EXCELLENT** - comprehensive competitor analysis, screen specs |
| Documentation | Superior `_memory/` folder with ADRs and design system |

---

## Issues Identified

### A. Dead Code & Artifacts (REMOVE)
| Item | Path | Action |
|------|------|--------|
| Genesis attempt logs | `genesis/attempt6202/chats/conversations.jsonl` | DELETE |
| Genesis directory | `genesis/` | DELETE entire directory |

### B. Stale Comments (UPDATE)
| File | Issue |
|------|-------|
| `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` | Lines 13-14 reference removed PatternFly migration |
| `apps/canvas/src/components/TimelineHeader.tsx` | Line 13 same stale comment |

### C. Documentation Inconsistencies (CONSOLIDATE)
| File | Issue | Action |
|------|-------|--------|
| `docs/SPECIFICATION.md` (63KB) | Original authoritative spec | KEEP as engineering reference |
| `docs/MMS_COMPLETE_SPECIFICATION.md` (295KB) | Extended version | REVIEW for consolidation |
| `docs/RESEARCH.md` | May reference old decisions | ARCHIVE to `docs/archive/` |
| `docs/PARALLEL_EXECUTION.md` | Old execution strategy | ARCHIVE |

### D. Missing UX Research (PORT FROM REFERENCE)
| Source File | Target Location | Content |
|-------------|-----------------|---------|
| `_memory/ux-competitor-analysis.md` | `docs/ux/competitor-analysis.md` | Competitor analysis (Maximo, Railnova, etc.) |
| `_memory/ux-screens.md` | `docs/ux/screens.md` | 7 screen specifications |
| `_memory/ux-charts.md` | `docs/ux/charts.md` | 9 chart type specifications |
| `_memory/ux-navigation.md` | `docs/ux/navigation.md` | User journeys, keyboard shortcuts |
| `_memory/ux-unknown-unknowns.md` | `docs/ux/unknown-unknowns.md` | Hidden requirements analysis |
| `_memory/design-system.md` | `docs/ux/design-system.md` | Color palette, tokens |
| `_memory/architecture-decisions.md` | `docs/architecture-decisions.md` | 5 ADRs |

---

## Realignment Tasks

### Phase 1: Cleanup Dead Code (15 min)
1. Delete `genesis/` directory entirely
2. Remove stale comments from InfiniteCanvas.tsx and TimelineHeader.tsx
3. Verify no broken imports after deletion

### Phase 2: Archive Stale Documentation (10 min)
1. Create `docs/archive/` directory
2. Move potentially outdated research docs:
   - `docs/RESEARCH.md` → `docs/archive/`
   - `docs/PARALLEL_EXECUTION.md` → `docs/archive/`
3. Update any internal links

### Phase 3: Port UX Research (30 min)
1. Create `docs/ux/` directory
2. Copy and adapt all `_memory/` files from reference project:
   - ux-competitor-analysis.md
   - ux-screens.md
   - ux-charts.md
   - ux-navigation.md
   - ux-unknown-unknowns.md
   - design-system.md
3. Copy architecture-decisions.md to docs/
4. Update file headers to reference current stack (BlueprintJS, not PatternFly)

### Phase 4: Consolidate Harness Files (15 min)
1. Review and update `features.json`:
   - Remove any stale/orphan feature entries
   - Ensure all 53 features are properly documented
2. Summarize `progress.txt`:
   - Archive historical sessions older than 30 days
   - Keep recent progress visible
3. Update `CLAUDE.md` with any new learnings

### Phase 5: Verification (20 min)
1. Run typecheck: `bun run typecheck`
2. Start dev server: `cd apps/canvas && bun run dev`
3. Visual verification via Playwright MCP:
   - All LOD levels render (MACRO/MESO/MICRO)
   - No console errors
   - 60fps+ maintained
4. Commit changes with descriptive message

---

## Stack Decision (REQUIRES CLARIFICATION)

### Current Divergence
| Aspect | Main Project | Reference |
|--------|--------------|-----------|
| React | 18.3.1 | 19.x |
| UI Library | BlueprintJS 6.1.0 | PatternFly 6 |
| R3F | 8.17.x | 9.x |

### Options
1. **KEEP Current Stack** (Recommended)
   - React 18 is stable with BlueprintJS 6
   - 53 features already working
   - Upgrade risk outweighs benefits for V1.0

2. **Upgrade to Reference Stack**
   - React 19 + PatternFly 6 + R3F 9
   - Breaking changes in all three
   - Would require significant refactoring

---

## Verification Checklist

After implementation:
- [ ] `genesis/` directory deleted
- [ ] No stale framework references in comments
- [ ] `docs/ux/` contains all research from reference
- [ ] `docs/archive/` contains old research docs
- [ ] Typecheck passes
- [ ] Visual verification passes (3 LOD levels)
- [ ] No console errors
- [ ] 60fps+ maintained
- [ ] features.json updated
- [ ] progress.txt updated
- [ ] Git commit created

---

## Files to Modify

### DELETE
- `genesis/` (entire directory)

### UPDATE
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (remove stale comments)
- `apps/canvas/src/components/TimelineHeader.tsx` (remove stale comments)
- `features.json` (cleanup if needed)
- `progress.txt` (summarize old sessions)
- `CLAUDE.md` (if needed)

### CREATE
- `docs/ux/competitor-analysis.md`
- `docs/ux/screens.md`
- `docs/ux/charts.md`
- `docs/ux/navigation.md`
- `docs/ux/unknown-unknowns.md`
- `docs/ux/design-system.md`
- `docs/architecture-decisions.md`
- `docs/archive/` (directory)

### MOVE
- `docs/RESEARCH.md` → `docs/archive/RESEARCH.md`
- `docs/PARALLEL_EXECUTION.md` → `docs/archive/PARALLEL_EXECUTION.md`

---

## Estimated Time: ~90 minutes

| Phase | Duration |
|-------|----------|
| Phase 1: Cleanup | 15 min |
| Phase 2: Archive | 10 min |
| Phase 3: Port UX | 30 min |
| Phase 4: Harness | 15 min |
| Phase 5: Verify | 20 min |

---

## Post-Realignment State

After this plan is executed:
1. Project will have NO dead code or artifacts
2. All valuable UX research will be consolidated in `docs/ux/`
3. Stale documentation will be archived (not deleted, for reference)
4. Harness files will be clean and current
5. Project will be ready for continued development on V1.0 features

    94→
    95→The **Entity in Charge of Maintenance (ECM)** framework establishes legal responsibilities for railway vehicle maintenance. While Dubai Metro is outside EU jurisdiction, the ECM framework represents industry best practice.
    96→
    97→#### ECM Function Mapping
    98→
    99→| Function | Responsibility | MMS V1.0 Feature | Status |
   100→|----------|----------------|------------------|--------|
   101→| **ECM-F1** | Maintenance Management | Dashboard KPIs, compliance tracking | Visualization only |
   102→| **ECM-F2** | Maintenance Development | Interval definitions, plan visualization | Mock data display |
   103→| **ECM-F3** | Fleet Maintenance Management | Real-time fleet status, conflict detection | Conflict indicators |
   104→| **ECM-F4** | Maintenance Delivery | Work order visualization, depot allocation | Visual representation |
   105→
   106→#### ECM Compliance Requirements
   107→
   108→| Requirement | ECM Article | MMS V1.0 Implementation |
   109→|-------------|-------------|------------------------|
   110→| Risk assessment | Art. 5.2 | Conflict detection, overdue warnings |
   111→| Competence management | Art. 5.3 | Not implemented |
   112→| Documentation | Art. 5.4 | Not implemented |
   113→| Performance monitoring | Art. 5.5 | KPI display |
   114→

# PART I: DOMAIN MODEL

## 1. Railway Maintenance Domain

### 1.1 ECM Framework (EU 2019/779)

The **Entity in Charge of Maintenance (ECM)** framework establishes legal responsibilities for railway vehicle maintenance. While Dubai Metro is outside EU jurisdiction, the ECM framework represents industry best practice.

#### ECM Function Mapping

| Function | Responsibility | MMS V1.0 Feature | Status |
|----------|----------------|------------------|--------|
| **ECM-F1** | Maintenance Management | Dashboard KPIs, compliance tracking | Visualization only |
| **ECM-F2** | Maintenance Development | Interval definitions, plan visualization | Mock data display |
| **ECM-F3** | Fleet Maintenance Management | Real-time fleet status, conflict detection | Conflict indicators |
| **ECM-F4** | Maintenance Delivery | Work order visualization, depot allocation | Visual representation |

#### ECM Compliance Requirements

| Requirement | ECM Article | MMS V1.0 Implementation |
|-------------|-------------|------------------------|
| Risk assessment | Art. 5.2 | Conflict detection, overdue warnings |
| Competence management | Art. 5.3 | Not implemented |
| Documentation | Art. 5.4 | Not implemented |
| Performance monitoring | Art. 5.5 | KPI display |

---

### 1.2 RAMS Compliance (EN 50126-1)

**RAMS** = Reliability, Availability, Maintainability, Safety

EN 50126-1:2017 defines the railway RAMS lifecycle. MMS visualizes RAMS metrics.

#### RAMS Metric Definitions

| Aspect | Definition | MMS V1.0 Feature |
|--------|------------|------------------|
| **Reliability** | Probability of intended function under stated conditions | MTBF display |
| **Availability** | Ability to perform required function at given instant | Fleet availability % |
| **Maintainability** | Probability of maintenance within stated time | MTTR display |
| **Safety** | Freedom from unacceptable risk | Critical task priority |

#### RAMS Targets

| Metric | Formula | Target | Critical Threshold |
|--------|---------|--------|-------------------|
| Fleet Availability | (Fit Trains / Total Trains) x 100 | >= 97% | < 95% |
| MTBF | Total Operating Hours / Number of Failures | Operator-defined | Operator-defined |
| MTTR | Total Downtime Hours / Number of Repairs | <= 24 hours | > 48 hours |
| Schedule Adherence | (On-Time / Scheduled) x 100 | >= 95% | < 90% |

---

### 1.3 EN 15380 Railway Terminology

All user-facing labels MUST use EN 15380 compliant terminology.

#### Mandatory Term Usage

| EN 15380 Term | Definition | NOT Acceptable |
|---------------|------------|----------------|
| Rolling Stock | Railway vehicles requiring maintenance | Vehicle, Asset, Unit |
| Fleet | Group of rolling stock sharing manufacturer/model | Group, Class, Collection |
| Depot | Designated maintenance facility | Garage, Workshop, Station |
| Train Fitness | Binary operational readiness (fit/unfit) | Availability, Health, Status |
| Run Assignment | Daily allocation of train IDs to routes | Dispatch, Schedule, Trip |
| Dead Running | Non-revenue movement between locations | Empty movement, Repositioning |
| Mileage Balancing | Strategy to equalize wear across fleet | Usage balancing |

#### Maintenance Terminology

| Term | Definition | Context |
|------|------------|---------|
| PM (Preventive Maintenance) | Scheduled maintenance at defined intervals | Time or mileage-based |
| CM (Corrective Maintenance) | Unscheduled repair after failure detection | Reactive |
| CBM (Condition-Based Maintenance) | Triggered by condition monitoring | Sensor-based |
| Overhaul | Major refurbishment restoring to original condition | Resets counters |
| Heavy Maintenance | Significant maintenance requiring extended depot stay | Multi-day duration |

---

## Session: 2026-01-05 (TOOLKIT-PERF-001: Complete Performance Audit) - COMPLETE ✅

### @zazcart/tools Critical Performance Fixes - ALL COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Complete audit and fix all performance issues in @zazcart/tools state machine library identified in FIXME files.

**Fixes Applied (7 total):**

1. **Unsafe Listener Unsubscription** (store.ts)
   - Copy listeners array before iteration to prevent issues during unsubscribe
   - Prevents ConcurrentModificationException-like bugs

2. **Validation on Hot Path** (context.ts)
   - Skip schema validation in production for performance
   - Only validate in development (NODE_ENV !== 'production')

3. **devLog Crash Risk** (logs.ts)
   - Safe error handling instead of throwing in devLog

4. **O(N²) reduceObject** (object-utils.ts)
   - Changed from spread operator in loop to Object.assign
   - O(N) implementation now

5. **GC Thrashing - Metadata Option** (machine.ts, models/machine.ts)
   - Added `metadata?: boolean` option to MachineConfig
   - When `false`, skips metadata updates on dispatch
   - Prevents GC thrashing from object allocations at 60fps

6. **V8 De-optimization** (hooks.ts)
   - Clean object return instead of hybrid array+object
   - Breaking change: `useMachine()` returns `{ state, actions, dispatch }`

7. **Double Proxying** (machine.ts)
   - Direct assignment instead of Immer produce() for selectors
   - Reduces proxy overhead in hot path

# MMS Project Realignment Plan

## Executive Summary

This plan addresses the cleanup and realignment of the MMS (Metro Mileage Management System) project at `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/mms/system`, incorporating valuable UX research from the reference project at `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/mms-last-attempt`.

**Key Finding**: The current project is MORE advanced (53 completed features vs 27 in reference), but the reference has superior UX research and documentation that should be ported.

| Aspect | Status |
|--------|--------|
| Features Complete | 53 of 53 (100%) |
| Stack | React 18.3.1 + BlueprintJS 6.1.0 + R3F 8.17 + Three.js 0.170 |

| Spatial Indexing | RBush (O(log n) queries) |
| Performance | 120fps verified |
| Storybook | **NONE** (no cleanup needed) |

| Aspect | Status |
|--------|--------|

| Documentation | Superior `_memory/` folder with ADRs and design system |

   117→### 1.2 RAMS Compliance (EN 50126-1)
   118→
   119→**RAMS** = Reliability, Availability, Maintainability, Safety
   120→
   121→EN 50126-1:2017 defines the railway RAMS lifecycle. MMS visualizes RAMS metrics.
   122→
   123→#### RAMS Metric Definitions
   124→
   125→| Aspect | Definition | MMS V1.0 Feature |
   126→|--------|------------|------------------|
   127→| **Reliability** | Probability of intended function under stated conditions | MTBF display |
   128→| **Availability** | Ability to perform required function at given instant | Fleet availability % |
   129→| **Maintainability** | Probability of maintenance within stated time | MTTR display |
   130→| **Safety** | Freedom from unacceptable risk | Critical task priority |
   131→
   132→#### RAMS Targets
   133→
   134→| Metric | Formula | Target | Critical Threshold |
   135→|--------|---------|--------|-------------------|
   136→| Fleet Availability | (Fit Trains / Total Trains) x 100 | >= 97% | < 95% |
   137→| MTBF | Total Operating Hours / Number of Failures | Operator-defined | Operator-defined |
   138→| MTTR | Total Downtime Hours / Number of Repairs | <= 24 hours | > 48 hours |
   139→| Schedule Adherence | (On-Time / Scheduled) x 100 | >= 95% | < 90% |
   140→

   143→### 1.3 EN 15380 Railway Terminology
   144→
   145→All user-facing labels MUST use EN 15380 compliant terminology.

   147→#### Mandatory Term Usage
   148→
   149→| EN 15380 Term | Definition | NOT Acceptable |
   150→|---------------|------------|----------------|
   151→| Rolling Stock | Railway vehicles requiring maintenance | Vehicle, Asset, Unit |
   152→| Fleet | Group of rolling stock sharing manufacturer/model | Group, Class, Collection |
   153→| Depot | Designated maintenance facility | Garage, Workshop, Station |
   154→| Train Fitness | Binary operational readiness (fit/unfit) | Availability, Health, Status |
   155→| Run Assignment | Daily allocation of train IDs to routes | Dispatch, Schedule, Trip |
   156→| Dead Running | Non-revenue movement between locations | Empty movement, Repositioning |
   157→| Mileage Balancing | Strategy to equalize wear across fleet | Usage balancing |
   158→
   159→#### Maintenance Terminology
   160→
   161→| Term | Definition | Context |
   162→|------|------------|---------|
   163→| PM (Preventive Maintenance) | Scheduled maintenance at defined intervals | Time or mileage-based |
   164→| CM (Corrective Maintenance) | Unscheduled repair after failure detection | Reactive |
   165→| CBM (Condition-Based Maintenance) | Triggered by condition monitoring | Sensor-based |
   166→| Overhaul | Major refurbishment restoring to original condition | Resets counters |
   167→| Heavy Maintenance | Significant maintenance requiring extended depot stay | Multi-day duration |

# PART I: DOMAIN MODEL

## 1. Railway Maintenance Domain

### 1.1 ECM Framework (EU 2019/779)

The **Entity in Charge of Maintenance (ECM)** framework establishes legal responsibilities for railway vehicle maintenance. While Dubai Metro is outside EU jurisdiction, the ECM framework represents industry best practice.

#### ECM Function Mapping

| Function | Responsibility | MMS V1.0 Feature | Status |
|----------|----------------|------------------|--------|
| **ECM-F1** | Maintenance Management | Dashboard KPIs, compliance tracking | Visualization only |
| **ECM-F2** | Maintenance Development | Interval definitions, plan visualization | Mock data display |
| **ECM-F3** | Fleet Maintenance Management | Real-time fleet status, conflict detection | Conflict indicators |
| **ECM-F4** | Maintenance Delivery | Work order visualization, depot allocation | Visual representation |

#### ECM Compliance Requirements

| Requirement | ECM Article | MMS V1.0 Implementation |
|-------------|-------------|------------------------|
| Risk assessment | Art. 5.2 | Conflict detection, overdue warnings |
| Competence management | Art. 5.3 | Not implemented |
| Documentation | Art. 5.4 | Not implemented |
| Performance monitoring | Art. 5.5 | KPI display |

---

### 1.2 RAMS Compliance (EN 50126-1)

**RAMS** = Reliability, Availability, Maintainability, Safety

EN 50126-1:2017 defines the railway RAMS lifecycle. MMS visualizes RAMS metrics.

#### RAMS Metric Definitions

| Aspect | Definition | MMS V1.0 Feature |
|--------|------------|------------------|
| **Reliability** | Probability of intended function under stated conditions | MTBF display |
| **Availability** | Ability to perform required function at given instant | Fleet availability % |
| **Maintainability** | Probability of maintenance within stated time | MTTR display |
| **Safety** | Freedom from unacceptable risk | Critical task priority |

#### RAMS Targets

| Metric | Formula | Target | Critical Threshold |
|--------|---------|--------|-------------------|
| Fleet Availability | (Fit Trains / Total Trains) x 100 | >= 97% | < 95% |
| MTBF | Total Operating Hours / Number of Failures | Operator-defined | Operator-defined |
| MTTR | Total Downtime Hours / Number of Repairs | <= 24 hours | > 48 hours |
| Schedule Adherence | (On-Time / Scheduled) x 100 | >= 95% | < 90% |

---

### 1.3 EN 15380 Railway Terminology

All user-facing labels MUST use EN 15380 compliant terminology.

#### Mandatory Term Usage

| EN 15380 Term | Definition | NOT Acceptable |
|---------------|------------|----------------|
| Rolling Stock | Railway vehicles requiring maintenance | Vehicle, Asset, Unit |
| Fleet | Group of rolling stock sharing manufacturer/model | Group, Class, Collection |
| Depot | Designated maintenance facility | Garage, Workshop, Station |
| Train Fitness | Binary operational readiness (fit/unfit) | Availability, Health, Status |
| Run Assignment | Daily allocation of train IDs to routes | Dispatch, Schedule, Trip |
| Dead Running | Non-revenue movement between locations | Empty movement, Repositioning |
| Mileage Balancing | Strategy to equalize wear across fleet | Usage balancing |

#### Maintenance Terminology

| Term | Definition | Context |
|------|------------|---------|
| PM (Preventive Maintenance) | Scheduled maintenance at defined intervals | Time or mileage-based |
| CM (Corrective Maintenance) | Unscheduled repair after failure detection | Reactive |
| CBM (Condition-Based Maintenance) | Triggered by condition monitoring | Sensor-based |
| Overhaul | Major refurbishment restoring to original condition | Resets counters |
| Heavy Maintenance | Significant maintenance requiring extended depot stay | Multi-day duration |

---

## Session: 2026-01-05 (TOOLKIT-PERF-001: Complete Performance Audit) - COMPLETE ✅

### @zazcart/tools Critical Performance Fixes - ALL COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Complete audit and fix all performance issues in @zazcart/tools state machine library identified in FIXME files.

**Fixes Applied (7 total):**

1. **Unsafe Listener Unsubscription** (store.ts)
   - Copy listeners array before iteration to prevent issues during unsubscribe
   - Prevents ConcurrentModificationException-like bugs

2. **Validation on Hot Path** (context.ts)
   - Skip schema validation in production for performance
   - Only validate in development (NODE_ENV !== 'production')

3. **devLog Crash Risk** (logs.ts)
   - Safe error handling instead of throwing in devLog

4. **O(N²) reduceObject** (object-utils.ts)
   - Changed from spread operator in loop to Object.assign
   - O(N) implementation now

5. **GC Thrashing - Metadata Option** (machine.ts, models/machine.ts)
   - Added `metadata?: boolean` option to MachineConfig
   - When `false`, skips metadata updates on dispatch
   - Prevents GC thrashing from object allocations at 60fps

6. **V8 De-optimization** (hooks.ts)
   - Clean object return instead of hybrid array+object
   - Breaking change: `useMachine()` returns `{ state, actions, dispatch }`

7. **Double Proxying** (machine.ts)
   - Direct assignment instead of Immer produce() for selectors
   - Reduces proxy overhead in hot path

# MMS Project Realignment Plan

## Executive Summary

This plan addresses the cleanup and realignment of the MMS (Metro Mileage Management System) project at `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/mms/system`, incorporating valuable UX research from the reference project at `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/mms-last-attempt`.

**Key Finding**: The current project is MORE advanced (53 completed features vs 27 in reference), but the reference has superior UX research and documentation that should be ported.

---

## Current State Analysis

### Main Project (mms/system)
| Aspect | Status |
|--------|--------|
| Features Complete | 53 of 53 (100%) |
| Stack | React 18.3.1 + BlueprintJS 6.1.0 + R3F 8.17 + Three.js 0.170 |
| State Management | @zazcart/tools state machine |
| Spatial Indexing | RBush (O(log n) queries) |
| Performance | 120fps verified |
| Storybook | **NONE** (no cleanup needed) |

### Reference Project (mms-last-attempt)
| Aspect | Status |
|--------|--------|
| Features Complete | 27 of 27 |
| Stack | React 19 + PatternFly 6 + R3F 9 + Three.js 0.170 |
| UX Research | **EXCELLENT** - comprehensive competitor analysis, screen specs |
| Documentation | Superior `_memory/` folder with ADRs and design system |

---

## Issues Identified

### A. Dead Code & Artifacts (REMOVE)
| Item | Path | Action |
|------|------|--------|
| Genesis attempt logs | `genesis/attempt6202/chats/conversations.jsonl` | DELETE |
| Genesis directory | `genesis/` | DELETE entire directory |

### B. Stale Comments (UPDATE)
| File | Issue |
|------|-------|
| `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` | Lines 13-14 reference removed PatternFly migration |
| `apps/canvas/src/components/TimelineHeader.tsx` | Line 13 same stale comment |

### C. Documentation Inconsistencies (CONSOLIDATE)
| File | Issue | Action |
|------|-------|--------|
| `docs/SPECIFICATION.md` (63KB) | Original authoritative spec | KEEP as engineering reference |
| `docs/MMS_COMPLETE_SPECIFICATION.md` (295KB) | Extended version | REVIEW for consolidation |
| `docs/RESEARCH.md` | May reference old decisions | ARCHIVE to `docs/archive/` |
| `docs/PARALLEL_EXECUTION.md` | Old execution strategy | ARCHIVE |

### D. Missing UX Research (PORT FROM REFERENCE)
| Source File | Target Location | Content |
|-------------|-----------------|---------|
| `_memory/ux-competitor-analysis.md` | `docs/ux/competitor-analysis.md` | Competitor analysis (Maximo, Railnova, etc.) |
| `_memory/ux-screens.md` | `docs/ux/screens.md` | 7 screen specifications |
| `_memory/ux-charts.md` | `docs/ux/charts.md` | 9 chart type specifications |

| `_memory/ux-unknown-unknowns.md` | `docs/ux/unknown-unknowns.md` | Hidden requirements analysis |
| `_memory/design-system.md` | `docs/ux/design-system.md` | Color palette, tokens |
| `_memory/architecture-decisions.md` | `docs/architecture-decisions.md` | 5 ADRs |

---

## Realignment Tasks

### Phase 1: Cleanup Dead Code (15 min)
1. Delete `genesis/` directory entirely
2. Remove stale comments from InfiniteCanvas.tsx and TimelineHeader.tsx
3. Verify no broken imports after deletion

### Phase 2: Archive Stale Documentation (10 min)
1. Create `docs/archive/` directory
2. Move potentially outdated research docs:
   - `docs/RESEARCH.md` → `docs/archive/`
   - `docs/PARALLEL_EXECUTION.md` → `docs/archive/`
3. Update any internal links

### Phase 3: Port UX Research (30 min)
1. Create `docs/ux/` directory
2. Copy and adapt all `_memory/` files from reference project:
   - ux-competitor-analysis.md
   - ux-screens.md
   - ux-charts.md
   - ux-navigation.md
   - ux-unknown-unknowns.md
   - design-system.md
3. Copy architecture-decisions.md to docs/
4. Update file headers to reference current stack (BlueprintJS, not PatternFly)

### Phase 4: Consolidate Harness Files (15 min)
1. Review and update `features.json`:
   - Remove any stale/orphan feature entries
   - Ensure all 53 features are properly documented
2. Summarize `progress.txt`:
   - Archive historical sessions older than 30 days
   - Keep recent progress visible
3. Update `CLAUDE.md` with any new learnings

### Phase 5: Verification (20 min)
1. Run typecheck: `bun run typecheck`
2. Start dev server: `cd apps/canvas && bun run dev`
3. Visual verification via Playwright MCP:
   - All LOD levels render (MACRO/MESO/MICRO)
   - No console errors
   - 60fps+ maintained
4. Commit changes with descriptive message

---

## Stack Decision (REQUIRES CLARIFICATION)

### Current Divergence
| Aspect | Main Project | Reference |
|--------|--------------|-----------|
| React | 18.3.1 | 19.x |
| UI Library | BlueprintJS 6.1.0 | PatternFly 6 |
| R3F | 8.17.x | 9.x |

### Options
1. **KEEP Current Stack** (Recommended)
   - React 18 is stable with BlueprintJS 6
   - 53 features already working
   - Upgrade risk outweighs benefits for V1.0

2. **Upgrade to Reference Stack**
   - React 19 + PatternFly 6 + R3F 9
   - Breaking changes in all three
   - Would require significant refactoring

---

import json

filepath = "/Users/anotonio.silva/.claude/projects/-Users-anotonio-silva-antonio-mms-system/34c1e0bd-3e55-46d7-a3d5-49f42992897f.jsonl"
tasks = []
errors = []
commits = []
actions = []

with open(filepath, 'r') as f:
    for i, line in enumerate(f):
        try:
            data = json.loads(line)
            content = data.get('content', '')
            role = data.get('role', '')
            
            # Extract commits
            if 'commit' in content.lower() or 'git' in content.lower():
                if any(marker in content for marker in ['git commit', 'refactor:', 'fix:', 'feat:', 'docs:']):
                    commits.append(content[:200])
            
            # Extract errors
            if any(err in content.lower() for err in ['error', 'failed', 'error:', 'failed:']):
                errors.append(content[:200])
            
            # Extract tasks (user messages)
            if role == 'user' and len(content) > 50:
                tasks.append(content[:150])
                
        except json.JSONDecodeError:
            continue

print(f"Total lines: {i+1}")
print(f"\nFirst 5 tasks requested:")
for t in tasks[:5]:
    print(f"  - {t}...")

print(f"\nFirst 5 errors encountered:")
for e in errors[:5]:
    print(f"  - {e}...")

print(f"\nFirst 5 commits made:")
for c in commits[:5]:
    print(f"  - {c}...")

python3 << 'PYEOF'
import json

filepath = "/Users/anotonio.silva/.claude/projects/-Users-anotonio-silva-antonio-mms-system/34c1e0bd-3e55-46d7-a3d5-49f42992897f.jsonl"
user_messages = []
assistant_actions = []
errors_found = []
commits_made = []

with open(filepath, 'r') as f:
    for i, line in enumerate(f):
        try:
            data = json.loads(line)
            msg_dict = data.get('message', {})
            msg_type = msg_dict.get('type', '')
            msg_content = msg_dict.get('content', '')
            record_type = data.get('type', '')
            
            if record_type == 'user':
                if msg_content:
                    user_messages.append({
                        'line': i,
                        'timestamp': data.get('timestamp', ''),
                        'content': msg_content[:400]
                    })
            
            elif record_type == 'assistant':
                if msg_type == 'tool_result':
                    # Look for errors and commits in tool results
                    if 'git commit' in msg_content or any(x in msg_content for x in ['refactor:', 'fix:', 'feat:', 'docs:', 'chore:']):
                        commits_made.append(msg_content[:300])
                    if 'error' in msg_content.lower() or 'failed' in msg_content.lower():
                        errors_found.append(msg_content[:300])
                elif msg_type == 'text':
                    assistant_actions.append(msg_content[:300])
                    
        except json.JSONDecodeError:
            continue

print(f"Total records: {i+1}")
print(f"User messages (tasks requested): {len(user_messages)}")
print(f"Assistant responses: {len(assistant_actions)}")
print(f"Errors found: {len(errors_found)}")
print(f"Commits made: {len(commits_made)}")

print("\n" + "="*70)
print("FIRST 3 TASKS REQUESTED BY USER:")
print("="*70)
for idx, msg in enumerate(user_messages[:3], 1):
    print(f"\n{idx}. [{msg['timestamp']}]")
    print(f"   {msg['content']}")
    
print("\n" + "="*70)
print("LAST 3 TASKS REQUESTED:")
print("="*70)
for idx, msg in enumerate(user_messages[-3:], 1):
    print(f"\n{idx}. [{msg['timestamp']}]")
    print(f"   {msg['content']}")

if errors_found:
    print("\n" + "="*70)
    print("ERRORS ENCOUNTERED:")
    print("="*70)
    for idx, err in enumerate(errors_found[:5], 1):
        print(f"\n{idx}. {err}")

if commits_made:
    print("\n" + "="*70)
    print("COMMITS MADE:")
    print("="*70)
    for idx, commit in enumerate(commits_made[:5], 1):
        print(f"\n{idx}. {commit}")

PYEOF

    89→# PART I: DOMAIN MODEL
    90→
    91→## 1. Railway Maintenance Domain
    92→
    93→### 1.1 ECM Framework (EU 2019/779)
    94→
    95→The **Entity in Charge of Maintenance (ECM)** framework establishes legal responsibilities for railway vehicle maintenance. While Dubai Metro is outside EU jurisdiction, the ECM framework represents industry best practice.
    96→
    97→#### ECM Function Mapping
    98→
    99→| Function | Responsibility | MMS V1.0 Feature | Status |
   100→|----------|----------------|------------------|--------|
   101→| **ECM-F1** | Maintenance Management | Dashboard KPIs, compliance tracking | Visualization only |
   102→| **ECM-F2** | Maintenance Development | Interval definitions, plan visualization | Mock data display |
   103→| **ECM-F3** | Fleet Maintenance Management | Real-time fleet status, conflict detection | Conflict indicators |
   104→| **ECM-F4** | Maintenance Delivery | Work order visualization, depot allocation | Visual representation |
   105→
   106→#### ECM Compliance Requirements
   107→
   108→| Requirement | ECM Article | MMS V1.0 Implementation |
   109→|-------------|-------------|------------------------|
   110→| Risk assessment | Art. 5.2 | Conflict detection, overdue warnings |
   111→| Competence management | Art. 5.3 | Not implemented |
   112→| Documentation | Art. 5.4 | Not implemented |
   113→| Performance monitoring | Art. 5.5 | KPI display |
   114→
   115→---
   116→
   117→### 1.2 RAMS Compliance (EN 50126-1)
   118→
   119→**RAMS** = Reliability, Availability, Maintainability, Safety
   120→
   121→EN 50126-1:2017 defines the railway RAMS lifecycle. MMS visualizes RAMS metrics.
   122→
   123→#### RAMS Metric Definitions
   124→
   125→| Aspect | Definition | MMS V1.0 Feature |
   126→|--------|------------|------------------|
   127→| **Reliability** | Probability of intended function under stated conditions | MTBF display |
   128→| **Availability** | Ability to perform required function at given instant | Fleet availability % |
   129→| **Maintainability** | Probability of maintenance within stated time | MTTR display |
   130→| **Safety** | Freedom from unacceptable risk | Critical task priority |
   131→
   132→#### RAMS Targets
   133→
   134→| Metric | Formula | Target | Critical Threshold |
   135→|--------|---------|--------|-------------------|
   136→| Fleet Availability | (Fit Trains / Total Trains) x 100 | >= 97% | < 95% |
   137→| MTBF | Total Operating Hours / Number of Failures | Operator-defined | Operator-defined |
   138→| MTTR | Total Downtime Hours / Number of Repairs | <= 24 hours | > 48 hours |
   139→| Schedule Adherence | (On-Time / Scheduled) x 100 | >= 95% | < 90% |
   140→
   141→---
   142→
   143→### 1.3 EN 15380 Railway Terminology
   144→
   145→All user-facing labels MUST use EN 15380 compliant terminology.
   146→
   147→#### Mandatory Term Usage
   148→
   149→| EN 15380 Term | Definition | NOT Acceptable |
   150→|---------------|------------|----------------|
   151→| Rolling Stock | Railway vehicles requiring maintenance | Vehicle, Asset, Unit |
   152→| Fleet | Group of rolling stock sharing manufacturer/model | Group, Class, Collection |
   153→| Depot | Designated maintenance facility | Garage, Workshop, Station |
   154→| Train Fitness | Binary operational readiness (fit/unfit) | Availability, Health, Status |
   155→| Run Assignment | Daily allocation of train IDs to routes | Dispatch, Schedule, Trip |
   156→| Dead Running | Non-revenue movement between locations | Empty movement, Repositioning |
   157→| Mileage Balancing | Strategy to equalize wear across fleet | Usage balancing |
   158→
   159→#### Maintenance Terminology
   160→
   161→| Term | Definition | Context |
   162→|------|------------|---------|
   163→| PM (Preventive Maintenance) | Scheduled maintenance at defined intervals | Time or mileage-based |
   164→| CM (Corrective Maintenance) | Unscheduled repair after failure detection | Reactive |
   165→| CBM (Condition-Based Maintenance) | Triggered by condition monitoring | Sensor-based |
   166→| Overhaul | Major refurbishment restoring to original condition | Resets counters |
   167→| Heavy Maintenance | Significant maintenance requiring extended depot stay | Multi-day duration |
   168→

   335→
   336→### 3.3 TaskRender (Computed)
   337→
   338→```typescript
   339→/**
   340→ * Color mapping for task types.
   341→ */
   342→export type TaskColor = 'blue' | 'orange' | 'gray';
   343→
   344→export const TYPE_TO_COLOR: Record<TaskType, TaskColor> = {
   345→  maintenance: 'blue',
   346→  overhaul: 'orange',
   347→  inspection: 'gray',
   348→};
   349→
   350→/**
   351→ * TaskRender - computed values for canvas rendering.
   352→ * NEVER persisted - recomputed on data load.
   353→ */
   354→export interface TaskRender {
   355→  id: string;
   356→  trainIndex: number;     // Y position (0-based index)
   357→  startDay: number;       // X position (days since start)
   358→  duration: number;       // Width in days
   359→  color: TaskColor;       // Derived from type
   360→  hasConflict: boolean;
   361→  type: TaskType;
   362→  predecessorId?: string;
   363→}
   364→
   365→/**
   366→ * Transform persistent Task to render-ready TaskRender.
   367→ */
   368→function transformToRender(
   369→  task: Task,
   370→  trainIndexMap: Map<string, number>,
   371→  datasetStartDate: string
   372→): TaskRender {
   373→  const trainIndex = trainIndexMap.get(task.trainId);
   374→  if (trainIndex === undefined) {
   375→    throw new Error(`Train not found: ${task.trainId}`);
   376→  }
   377→
   378→  const startDay = daysBetween(datasetStartDate, task.startDate);
   379→  const duration = daysBetween(task.startDate, task.endDate);
   380→
   381→  return {
   382→    id: task.id,
   383→    trainIndex,
   384→    startDay,
   385→    duration,
   386→    color: TYPE_TO_COLOR[task.type],
   387→    hasConflict: task.hasConflict,
   388→    type: task.type,
   389→    predecessorId: task.predecessorId,
   390→  };
   391→}
   392→```
   393→
   394→---
   395→
   396→### 3.4 Conflict Representation
   397→
   398→#### Conflict Detection Formula
   399→
   400→```
   401→Overlap Conflict:
   402→  (A.startDay <= B.startDay + B.duration) AND
   403→  (A.startDay + A.duration >= B.startDay) AND
   404→  (A.trainId == B.trainId)
   405→```
   406→
   407→#### Conflict Visualization
   408→
   409→| LOD Level | Conflict Indicator |
   410→|-----------|-------------------|
   411→| MACRO | Red intensity in heatmap cells |
   412→| MESO | Red background on task block |
   413→| MICRO | Red border + warning icon on task card |
   414→
   415→Color specification: Intent.DANGER red (`#cd4246`)

   476→---
   477→
   478→## 4. Mathematical Formulas
   479→
   480→### 4.1 Km Variance (Coefficient of Variation)
   481→
   482→Primary metric for fleet mileage balance. Lower variance = better distribution.
   483→
   484→```
   485→Km Variance % = (Standard Deviation / Mean) * 100
   486→
   487→Where:
   488→  mean = sum(all train km) / n
   489→  stddev = sqrt(sum((x_i - mean)^2) / n)
   490→```
   491→
   492→```typescript
   493→function calculateKmVariance(kmValues: number[]): number {
   494→  if (kmValues.length < 2) return 0;
   495→
   496→  const mean = kmValues.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / kmValues.length;
   497→  if (mean === 0) return 0;
   498→
   499→  const squaredDiffs = kmValues.map(km => Math.pow(km - mean, 2));
   500→  const variance = squaredDiffs.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / kmValues.length;
   501→  const stdDev = Math.sqrt(variance);
   502→
   503→  return (stdDev / mean) * 100;
   504→}
   505→```
   506→
   507→| Status | Variance | Indicator |
   508→|--------|----------|-----------|
   509→| Green (Healthy) | <= 10% | Green badge |
   510→| Yellow (Warning) | 10% - 15% | Yellow badge |
   511→| Red (Critical) | > 15% | Red badge |
   512→
   513→---
   514→
   515→### 4.2 Fleet Availability
   516→
   517→```
   518→Fleet Availability % = (Fit Trains / Total Trains) * 100
   519→
   520→Targets:
   521→  Target: >= 97%
   522→  Critical: < 95%
   523→```
   524→
   525→```typescript
   526→function calculateFleetAvailability(fitTrains: number, totalTrains: number): number {
   527→  if (totalTrains === 0) return 0;
   528→  return (fitTrains / totalTrains) * 100;
   529→}
   530→```
   531→
   532→---
   533→
   534→### 4.3 MTBF / MTTR / MDBF
   535→
   536→```
   537→MTBF = Total Operating Hours / Number of Failures (Hours)
   538→MDBF = Total Operating km / Number of Failures (Kilometers)
   539→MTTR = Total Downtime Hours / Number of Repairs (Hours)
   540→       Target: <= 24 hours, Critical: > 48 hours
   541→```
   542→
   543→```typescript
   544→interface FailureMetrics {
   545→  mtbf: number;
   546→  mdbf: number;
   547→  mttr: number;
   548→}
   549→
   550→function calculateFailureMetrics(
   551→  operatingHours: number,
   552→  operatingKm: number,
   553→  downtimeHours: number,
   554→  failureCount: number,
   555→  repairCount: number
   556→): FailureMetrics {
   557→  return {
   558→    mtbf: failureCount > 0 ? operatingHours / failureCount : Infinity,
   559→    mdbf: failureCount > 0 ? operatingKm / failureCount : Infinity,
   560→    mttr: repairCount > 0 ? downtimeHours / repairCount : 0,
   561→  };
   562→}
   563→```
   564→
   565→---
   566→
   567→### 4.4 Coordinate System
   568→
   569→#### Constants
   570→
   571→| Constant | Value | Description |
   572→|----------|-------|-------------|
   573→| `PX_PER_DAY` | 10 pixels | 1 day = 10 pixels on X axis |
   574→| `PX_PER_TRAIN` | 24 pixels | 1 train row = 24 pixels on Y axis |
   575→| `minZoom` | 0.1 | Minimum zoom (zoomed out) |
   576→| `maxZoom` | 5.0 | Maximum zoom (zoomed in) |
   577→
   578→#### Coordinate Conversions
   579→
   580→```typescript
   581→const PX_PER_DAY = 10;
   582→const PX_PER_TRAIN = 24;
   583→
   584→function dayToX(day: number): number {
   585→  return day * PX_PER_DAY;
   586→}
   587→
   588→function trainIndexToY(index: number): number {
   589→  return -index * PX_PER_TRAIN;
   590→}
   591→
   592→function screenToWorld(
   593→  screenX: number, screenY: number,
   594→  cameraX: number, cameraY: number,
   595→  zoom: number,
   596→  viewportWidth: number, viewportHeight: number
   597→): { worldX: number; worldY: number } {
   598→  return {
   599→    worldX: cameraX + (screenX - viewportWidth / 2) / zoom,
   600→    worldY: cameraY + (screenY - viewportHeight / 2) / zoom,
   601→  };
   602→}
   603→```
   604→
   605→#### LOD Thresholds
   606→
   607→| LOD Level | Zoom Range | Time Scale | Rendering |
   608→|-----------|------------|------------|-----------|
   609→| MACRO | < 0.5 | 10-20 years | Heatmap (aggregated) |
   610→| MESO | 0.5 - 2.0 | 2-5 years | Task blocks (instanced) |
   611→| MICRO | >= 2.0 | 4-8 weeks | Detailed cards (HTML) |
   612→
   613→#### Blend Zone
   614→
   615→```typescript
   616→const BLEND_ZONE_SIZE = 0.15;
   617→
   618→function calculateOpacity(
   619→  zoom: number,
   620→  threshold: number,
   621→  direction: 'in' | 'out'
   622→): number {
   623→  const t = (zoom - threshold) / BLEND_ZONE_SIZE;
   624→  const clamped = Math.max(0, Math.min(1, t));
   625→  return direction === 'in' ? clamped : 1 - clamped;
   626→}
   627→```
   628→
   629→---
   630→
   631→### 4.5 Km Penalty Calculation (Solver)
   632→
   633→Used by allocation solver to prioritize under-utilized trains.
   634→
   635→```
   636→deviation_pct = |current_km - target_km| / max(target_km, 1) * 100
   637→
   638→If current_km < target_km:
   639→  penalty = -deviation_pct * 10  (BONUS)
   640→If current_km >= target_km:
   641→  penalty = +deviation_pct * 10  (PENALTY)
   642→```
   643→
   644→```typescript
   645→function calculateKmPenalty(
   646→  currentKm: number,
   647→  targetKm: number,
   648→  fleetAvgKm: number
   649→): number {
   650→  const effectiveTarget = targetKm || fleetAvgKm;
   651→  if (effectiveTarget === 0) return 0;
   652→
   653→  const deviationPct = Math.abs(currentKm - effectiveTarget) / effectiveTarget * 100;
   654→
   655→  if (currentKm < effectiveTarget) {
   656→    return -Math.round(deviationPct * 10);
   657→  } else {
   658→    return Math.round(deviationPct * 10);
   659→  }
   660→}
   661→```
   662→
   663→---
   664→
   665→### 4.6 Haversine Distance (Dead Running)
   666→
   667→Great circle distance between two geographic points.
   668→
   669→```
   670→a = sin^2(delta_lat/2) + cos(lat1) * cos(lat2) * sin^2(delta_lon/2)
   671→c = 2 * atan2(sqrt(a), sqrt(1-a))
   672→distance = R * c
   673→
   674→Where: R = 6371 km (Earth's radius)
   675→```
   676→
   677→```typescript
   678→function haversineDistance(
   679→  lat1: number, lon1: number,
   680→  lat2: number, lon2: number
   681→): number {
   682→  const R = 6371;
   683→  const toRad = (deg: number) => deg * Math.PI / 180;
   684→
   685→  const lat1Rad = toRad(lat1);
   686→  const lat2Rad = toRad(lat2);
   687→  const deltaLat = toRad(lat2 - lat1);
   688→  const deltaLon = toRad(lon2 - lon1);
   689→
   690→  const a = Math.sin(deltaLat / 2) ** 2 +
   691→            Math.cos(lat1Rad) * Math.cos(lat2Rad) *
   692→            Math.sin(deltaLon / 2) ** 2;
   693→
   694→  const c = 2 * Math.atan2(Math.sqrt(a), Math.sqrt(1 - a));
   695→
   696→  return R * c;
   697→}
   698→```
   699→
   700→---

   702→## 5. Maintenance Level Mapping
   703→
   704→### Authoritative Mapping Table
   705→
   706→| Code | Railway Term | Display | Interval (km) | Tolerance | Duration | Man-Hours | Tracks | Cost |
   707→|------|--------------|---------|---------------|-----------|----------|-----------|--------|------|
   708→| A | PM1 | A-Check | 10,000 | +/- 2,000 | 4h | 8 | 1 | $2,500 |
   709→| B | PM2 | B-Check | 50,000 | +/- 5,000 | 16h | 32 | 1 | $8,500 |
   710→| C | PM3 | C-Check | 200,000 | +/- 20,000 | 72h | 160 | 1 | $45,000 |
   711→| D | HM | D-Overhaul | 800,000 | +/- 50,000 | 480h | 1,200 | 2 | $180,000 |
   712→
   713→#### Terminology Key
   714→
   715→| Term | Meaning |
   716→|------|---------|
   717→| **PM1** | Preventive Maintenance Level 1 (Light) |
   718→| **PM2** | Preventive Maintenance Level 2 (Intermediate) |
   719→| **PM3** | Preventive Maintenance Level 3 (Scheduled) |
   720→| **HM** | Heavy Maintenance / Major Overhaul |
   721→| **OVH** | Full Overhaul / Refurbishment (resets all counters) |
   722→
   723→### Maintenance Cascade
   724→
   725→```
   726→Fleet Type (e.g., Alstom Red Line)
   727→ |
   728→ +-- Maintenance Profile
   729→     |
   730→     +-- PM1 / A-Check (every 10,000 km)
   731→     |   Duration: 4 hours
   732→     |
   733→     +-- PM2 / B-Check (every 50,000 km)
   734→     |   Duration: 16 hours, INCLUDES PM1 tasks
   735→     |
   736→     +-- PM3 / C-Check (every 200,000 km)
   737→     |   Duration: 72 hours, INCLUDES PM1 + PM2 tasks
   738→     |
   739→     +-- HM / D-Overhaul (every 800,000 km)
   740→         Duration: 480 hours, RESETS counters
   741→```
   742→
   743→### Maintenance Triggers
   744→
   745→| Trigger Type | Description | Best For |
   746→|--------------|-------------|----------|
   747→| Time-based | Calendar intervals | Simple assets, regulatory |
   748→| Mileage-based | Odometer intervals (primary) | Wear-dependent components |
   749→| Condition-based | Sensor thresholds | Critical components |
   750→| Hybrid | Combined triggers | Most applications |
   751→

   754→## 6. Domain Rules
   755→
   756→### 6.1 Hard Constraints (Never Violate)
   757→
   758→| ID | Rule | Rationale |
   759→|----|------|-----------|
   760→| HC-1 | Unfit trains NEVER assigned to service | Safety-critical |
   761→| HC-2 | Maintenance windows are immutable | Planned downtime |
   762→| HC-3 | Aptitude requirements are binary | Route compatibility |
   763→| HC-4 | Depot capacity is physical | Track slots |
   764→| HC-5 | Service cannot be cancelled for balancing | Operations > Optimization |
   765→
   766→### 6.2 Soft Constraints (Optimize Within)
   767→
   768→| ID | Rule | Weight |
   769→|----|------|--------|
   770→| SC-1 | Km balancing priority | 1000 (HIGH) |
   771→| SC-2 | Dead running minimization | 100 (MEDIUM) |
   772→| SC-3 | Maintenance depot proximity | 10 (LOW) |
   773→
   774→### 6.3 Tolerance Windows
   775→
   776→| Level | Tolerance | Overrun Warning | Critical |
   777→|-------|-----------|-----------------|----------|
   778→| PM1 / A-Check | +/- 2,000 km | 95% | 105% |
   779→| PM2 / B-Check | +/- 5,000 km | 95% | 105% |
   780→| PM3 / C-Check | +/- 20,000 km | 95% | 105% |
   781→| HM / D-Overhaul | +/- 50,000 km | 95% | 105% |

   842→### Time Zone Concept (Frozen Periods)
   843→
   844→| Horizon | Zone | Constraint | Allowed Changes |
   845→|---------|------|------------|-----------------|
   846→| T > 12 weeks | **Liquid** | Soft | Structure, Volume, Frequency |
   847→| T-12 to T-7 | **Slushy** | Medium | Timing, Resource Allocation |
   848→| T-7 to T-48h | **Frozen** | Hard | Emergency Only |
